world.title: Hundreds missing after Rohingya boat capsizes in Andaman Sea: UN
world.summary: About 250 Rohingya refugees and Bangladeshi nationals are feared missing after a boat capsized in the Andaman Sea, according to the United Nations. The vessel, carrying men, women, and children, reportedly sank due to heavy winds, rough seas, and overcrowding. Search and rescue efforts are ongoing as authorities work to locate the missing individuals amid challenging conditions.

world.title: Jaishankar discusses Iran, Hormuz and Lebanon with Israel's Sa'ar
world.summary: Indian External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar discussed regional issues, including Iran, the Strait of Hormuz, and Lebanon, in a call with Israeli Minister Eli Sa'ar. Sa'ar emphasized that Iran's actions threaten navigation freedom and require a response. Jaishankar also spoke with Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong, addressing the ongoing conflict involving Iran and the US-Israel alliance.

world.title: Iran talks 2.0 may happen in next two days in Pak, says Trump. Then praises Munir
world.summary: US President Donald Trump indicated a potential second round of talks with Iran could occur within two days in Pakistan. His comments come amid rising tensions following the US blockade of Iranian ports, with Tehran threatening regional strikes. The situation in the Strait of Hormuz raises concerns about renewed hostilities and the economic impact of the ongoing regional conflict.

business.title: Gold surges as Iran talks hopes dent US Dollar again
business.summary: Gold (XAU/USD) prices increased nearly 2% on Tuesday, reaching $4,835 after recovering from daily lows of $4,742. This surge is attributed to rising optimism surrounding the resumption of US-Iran talks. Despite this, the US military continues to seize Iran-linked ships amid an ongoing blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, impacting regional tensions and market dynamics.

business.title: Trade Spotlight: How should you trade Engineers India, Sona Comstar, Lenskart, KRN Heat Exchanger, TD Power Systems, Bharti Airtel, and others on April 15?
business.summary: The market is anticipated to rebound on April 15, driven by optimism surrounding potential US-Iran peace talks. Analysts have provided a list of short-term trading ideas, including recommendations for stocks like Engineers India, Sona Comstar, Lenskart, KRN Heat Exchanger, TD Power Systems, and Bharti Airtel. Investors are encouraged to consider these suggestions for potential trading opportunities.

business.title: Tata invests ₹1,500 crore more to charge up iPhone biz
business.summary: Tata Group has invested ₹1,500 crore in Tata Electronics to enhance its iPhone manufacturing capabilities and plans additional investments. The group has also increased funding for its Pegatron India unit. Analysts indicate this strategy signifies a long-term capacity expansion, as Tata aims to strengthen its position within Apple's supply chains and expand its involvement in the semiconductor sector.

technology.title: New material may help aluminium batteries last longer, cost less
technology.summary: Scientists in Bengaluru have made progress in developing aluminium batteries as a safer and cheaper alternative to lithium-ion batteries. A research team, led by Kavita Patil, has addressed a longstanding issue that has hindered the performance of aluminium batteries. This advancement could potentially enhance battery longevity and reduce costs, marking a significant step forward in battery technology.

technology.title: GoPro Launches Mission 1 Pro with 8K Video and Advanced Features
technology.summary: GoPro has launched the Mission 1 Pro, part of its new Mission 1 series, which includes features like 8K video and 50MP photos. This camera targets the action cinema market, aiming to elevate the capabilities of action cameras by incorporating advanced features typically found in professional filming equipment. The Mission 1 Pro seeks to redefine the action camera experience.

technology.title: GoPro Unveils Mission 1 Lineup with 8K Video and Pro-Grade Interchangeable Lenses
technology.summary: GoPro has launched its Mission 1 lineup of action cameras, which includes the Mission 1, Mission 1 Pro, and Mission 1 Pro ILS. Each model features a large 1-inch sensor capable of recording 8K video and offers enhanced low-light performance. The new cameras are designed for professional use, incorporating interchangeable lenses for greater versatility in shooting.

sports.title: ”We’ll have to put four or six on him’ - Diego Simeone responds to Lamine Yamal’s challenge ahead of Barcelona’s clash with Atletico
sports.summary: Diego Simeone humorously suggested he might assign “four or six men” to mark Barcelona's Lamine Yamal ahead of their Champions League quarter-final second leg. This comment followed Yamal's pre-match remarks, where he expressed curiosity about whether Simeone would give him special attention during the game. The match highlights the growing prominence of the young Barcelona player.

sports.title: IPL 2026: Noor Ahmad's trickery stands out as CSK hand KKR fourth defeat in 5 games
sports.summary: Chennai Super Kings defeated Kolkata Knight Riders by 32 runs in the IPL, marking KKR's fourth loss in five matches. Batting first, CSK set a target of 193 runs. KKR struggled on a challenging pitch, finishing their innings at 160/7. Noor Ahmad's performance was notable as CSK capitalized on KKR's difficulties to secure the victory.

sports.title: Rohit skips MI nets but no information on tear in hamstring
sports.summary: Rohit Sharma did not attend the Mumbai Indians' practice session on April 14, raising concerns about his fitness ahead of the upcoming match against Punjab Kings. The team management has not confirmed the status of his hamstring injury, leaving his availability uncertain. They may choose to prioritize caution or allow Rohit to make the final decision regarding his participation.

health.title: When antibiotics fail: The hidden cost of self-medication
health.summary: Misuse of antibiotics during self-medication can lead to stronger infections, as improper use trains bacteria to resist treatment. A patient reported treating his flu-like symptoms with antibiotics without consulting a doctor, highlighting the risks associated with self-prescribing. This behavior contributes to the growing problem of antibiotic resistance, posing a significant public health challenge and increasing future treatment difficulties.

health.title: Childhood cancer in India on the rise: Expert warns late diagnosis is reducing survival rates among children
health.summary: Childhood cancer cases in India are increasing, with experts highlighting that late diagnosis is significantly reducing survival rates among affected children. They emphasize the importance of early detection and increased awareness to improve outcomes. Efforts to enhance screening and education about childhood cancer are crucial for saving more young lives in the country.

health.title: The gut microbiome as a fingerprint of antibiotic use history
health.summary: A study involving 14,979 individuals found that antibiotic use over eight years significantly altered gut microbiome composition. Data from the Swedish National Prescribed Drug Register and stool metagenomic profiles were analyzed. Changes occurred after just one course of antibiotics and varied widely among different antibiotic classes, highlighting the lasting impact of antibiotic exposure on gut health.

science.title: Is Yellowstone volcano near eruption? New study sparks buzz, scientists urge caution
science.summary: A recent study indicates that magma may be closer to the surface beneath Yellowstone, raising concerns about a potential eruption. However, scientists caution that fears of an imminent eruption are likely overstated. The study has generated significant online discussion, but experts emphasize the need for careful interpretation of the findings and a measured response to the information presented.

science.title: Study Links Excessive Screentime To Eating Disorders
science.summary: A recent study published in the Journal of Medical Internet Research indicates that excessive smartphone use is a significant risk factor for developing eating disorders. While health experts assert that smartphones are not the direct cause of these disorders, they can negatively impact eating habits. This finding highlights the broader implications of screen time beyond eye strain and sleep disruption.

science.title: This supermassive black hole is located in an unusual part of its galaxy
science.summary: Astronomers using the James Webb Space Telescope have identified a supermassive black hole in an unusual area of the elliptical galaxy NGC 4486B. This black hole has a mass approximately 360 million times that of the Sun. The discovery challenges existing theories, prompting researchers to propose a new hypothesis to explain its unexpected location within the galaxy.

entertainment.title: Sir one more chance please: Buchi Babu’s comment on Ram Charan’s Insta post goes viral
entertainment.summary: Ram Charan is currently filming the sports action drama "Peddi," directed by Buchi Babu Sana. The film's release, originally scheduled for April 30, has been postponed without a new date announced. Recently, Buchi Babu's comment on Ram Charan's Instagram post gained attention, contributing to the ongoing buzz surrounding the film and its lead actor in Telugu cinema news.

entertainment.title: Here Are All The K-Pop Artists Nominated For The 2026 American Music Awards
entertainment.summary: The 2026 American Music Awards (AMAs) announced its nominees on April 14. K-Pop artists received nominations based on fan interactions, including streaming and album sales. The AMAs aim to recognize popular music achievements, and this year's nominations highlight the growing influence of K-Pop in the global music industry. The full list of nominees includes various artists across multiple categories.

entertainment.title: Bobby Deol recalls Dharmendra’s struggle with alcohol; says his loss brought him closer to Esha Deol and Ahana Deol
entertainment.summary: Bobby Deol has shared his journey of quitting alcohol, a significant personal change he made a year and a half ago. He found the transition easier than anticipated. Deol also reflected on his father, Dharmendra's struggles with alcohol, noting that his father's loss strengthened his relationships with his sisters, Esha Deol and Ahana Deol.
